page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m1.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. The instance features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with a single thread per core, running on an Intel Xeon processor clocked at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 12.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server type does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. It has no integrated GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps. The hardware profile is characterized by a low memory-to-core ratio, prioritizing raw CPU core allocation over memory capacity.

Due to the 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to memory, this instance is suited for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. Appropriate use cases include batch processing, distributed compute nodes, and CPU-bound microservices. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration makes it less suitable for database hosting or machine learning workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2i-c1m1.3xlarge is an x86_64 virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core, paired with 12.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to RAM, making it highly compute-centric. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Qualitatively, this server provides a dense allocation of physical CPU cores relative to memory, offering cost efficiency for workloads that require parallel processing power without large memory overhead. It is best suited for CPU-bound tasks, batch processing, and distributed computing nodes.

tagline: A dedicated twelve-core compute instance with a one-to-one processor-to-memory ratio and eight gigabits per second baseline network bandwidth.
